examples: Agar plates; liquid culture; slants; culture syringes
assumption_audit: Good class; keep separate from general materials because strain identity and generation history matter
implementation_notes: Create items by species/strain and format when traceability matters

examples: Agar; light malt extract; dextrose; yeast extract; peptone
assumption_audit: Missing from original list; these are not substrate materials and should have their own purchasing/QC defaults
implementation_notes: Use in culture media BOMs and lab prep formulas

examples: Petri dishes; syringes; needles; scalpel blades; parafilm; gloves; alcohol wipes
assumption_audit: Missing from original list; decide which are true inventory versus expensed shop supplies
implementation_notes: Track only higher-value or stockout-critical consumables in ERP

examples: Rye; millet; sorghum; wheat; calcium carbonate; gypsum
assumption_audit: Missing as a separate class; grain for spawn has different BOMs and controls than bulk substrate
implementation_notes: Use for master spawn and production spawn formulas

examples: Grain master; sawdust master

implementation_notes: Track generation passage parent culture and use-by date

examples: Grain spawn; sawdust spawn; plug spawn
assumption_audit: Original spawn class is valid but should be named clearly so it does not include masters
implementation_notes: Use as component in inoculated substrate BOMs

examples: Lime; hardwood sawdust; soy hulls; straw; bran; gypsum; water
assumption_audit: Good class; include water only if the ERP will use it for formulas costing or moisture balance
implementation_notes: Separate food-grade and non-food-grade inputs if applicable

examples: Unicorn grow bags; filter patches; collars; plugs
assumption_audit: Original packaging raw materials example fits here better than finished product packaging
implementation_notes: Use in sterilized substrate and fruiting block BOMs

examples: Sterilized sawdust blocks; sterilized grain bags
assumption_audit: Good class if inventory is held between sterilization and inoculation; otherwise it can be just an operation output
implementation_notes: Track sterilization cycle equipment load and release status

examples: Inoculated sawdust blocks; inoculated grain bags
assumption_audit: Good class but define the boundary from sterilized substrate and fruiting blocks
implementation_notes: Track inoculation date spawn lot and expected incubation window

examples: Colonized shiitake block; lion's mane fruiting block
assumption_audit: Original mushroom fruiting blocks class is valid; use fruiting block as the item class name
implementation_notes: Move from inoculated substrate when blocks enter fruiting or are released to fruiting inventory

examples: Fresh lion's mane; fresh oyster; fresh shiitake
assumption_audit: Good class; fresh mushrooms can be both sellable finished goods and inputs to processing
implementation_notes: Use grade size harvest date and storage condition attributes

examples: Trim; seconds; unsold fresh mushrooms for drying
assumption_audit: Missing from original list; this prevents usable trim and seconds from disappearing into waste
implementation_notes: Use for powders extracts compost decisions and yield analysis

examples: Dried slices; dried whole fruiting bodies
assumption_audit: Missing if powders are made from an intermediate drying step
implementation_notes: Use as input to milling powder or as sellable dried product

examples: Lion's mane powder; reishi powder; blend powder

implementation_notes: Track mesh size species part and processing lot

examples: Tincture base; hot water extract; alcohol extract; concentrate

examples: Honey; cacao; sugar; salt; citric acid; glycerin; alcohol; carrier oil

examples: Capsules; tinctures; gummies; seasoning blends; drink mixes

examples: Product labels; lot stickers; inserts; case labels

examples: Sanitizer; detergent; bleach; isopropyl alcohol; mop heads

examples: Swabs; test strips; plates; reagents; sample bags
